description: Valuation Services Web API and associated production workflows.

components.name: Common Service API
components.description: Handles shared valuation operations including MPAC image retrieval.
investigationHints: Validate the MPAC image identifier and request parameters.
Check whether the returned image byte array is null, empty, truncated, or malformed.
Review code paths that create an image from a stream or byte array.
Search all logs using the request or correlation ID.
Validate downstream MPAC connectivity and response content.

components.name: ACI API
components.description: Handles ACI submission and audit operations.
investigationHints: Validate the request payload submitted to ACI.
Check the downstream ACI response and HTTP status.
Review AuditLog database writes for the same correlation ID.
Check database connectivity and SQL exception details.

workflows.name: MPAC Image Retrieval
workflows.description: Retrieves an image from MPAC and converts it into the format expected by the calling valuation workflow.
steps: Validate the property or image identifier.
Receive image content or encoded image data.
Convert the response into an image or byte array.
Return the result to the calling workflow.
investigationHints: Confirm which workflow supplied the property or image identifier.
Compare successful and failed MPAC responses.
Validate content type, content length, and image encoding.
Inspect image conversion code for System.ArgumentException.

workflows.name: ACI Submission
workflows.description: Submits valuation information to the ACI integration and records the operation in the audit log.
steps: Return the result to the caller.
investigationHints: Validate the incoming ACI request payload.
Check whether the failure occurred before or after the downstream call.
Review AuditLog writes and database exceptions.
Compare correlation IDs across API, database, and downstream logs.

knownIssues.title: Invalid MPAC image content
knownIssues.description: The MPAC image endpoint can throw System.ArgumentException when invalid or incomplete image content is passed to the image-conversion library.
likelyCauses: The downstream MPAC response contains invalid image bytes.
The image byte array is empty or truncated.
The requested MPAC image does not exist.
The response contains an error payload instead of image content.
The stream position is incorrect before image conversion.
investigationSteps: Locate the request using the correlation ID.
Capture the MPAC response content type and content length.
Confirm that the response contains image data rather than JSON or HTML.
Validate the image bytes before passing them into the image library.
Compare the failed request with a successful request.
resolutionSteps: Add response validation before image conversion.
Reject empty or malformed image content with a controlled application error.
Handle downstream MPAC error responses separately.
Add logging for image identifier, response status, content type, and content length.
suggestedQueries: Search logs using the correlation ID from the failed request.
Query AuditLog for the correlation ID and API path.
Find other getMpacImages failures with the same image identifier.

knownIssues.title: AuditLog database failure
knownIssues.description: The application failed while reading or writing ACI audit information.
likelyCauses: A database encryption key or certificate is unavailable.
The application database user lacks permission to open the key.
The database connection is pointing to an incorrect environment.
The encryption key was not opened within the current database session.
investigationSteps: Confirm the database and server used by the application instance.
Review the complete SQL exception and inner exception.
Validate certificate and symmetric-key permissions for the application account.
Run the affected query using the same database identity where possible.
Check recent database deployment or permission changes.
resolutionSteps: Correct the database connection if it points to the wrong environment.
Open the required key before executing the encrypted query.
Coordinate with the DBA when certificate or key ownership must be corrected.
suggestedQueries: Search database logs for key or certificate errors.
Search application logs using the same correlation ID.
Review recent database permission and deployment changes.
